Title: The Other Side of the Table
Setting: Neutral conference site, {location} financial district, security personnel from both sides watching each other watch each other
TextLines: They asked for the meet. You didn't expect them to bring lawyers and an envoy with a rank you can't quite parse.
"This isn't a negotiation," the envoy says, before anyone has spoken. "This is a notification."
{operative.role:anchor} is at your shoulder, hand close to where a sidearm would be if security hadn't taken everyone's at the door.
"You've been operating against our interests for six cycles. We've documented it. We're going to start responding."
There's no anger in their voice. That's worse than anger. Anger you can negotiate with.
"You have the rest of this meeting to tell us why we shouldn't."

Choices.Label: "What would it cost to make this go away?"
"Respond however you want. We'll meet it."
"What did we do that you noticed? Specifically."
